title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility · Behavior Case: Structured Alternative
summary: The report encodes meaning only with color, clips long words, changes columns by terminal width, mixes diagnostics with JSON stdout, and gives screen-reader users an unlabeled number grid. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the structured alternative increment in accessible human and machine report system. Lear…
objectives: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.
Keep every status and distinction understandable without color and honor plain or no-color operation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-report-format-accessibility-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-report-format-accessibility-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_report_format_accessibility_5(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"

# Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-641sry
# Competency: bookbot-structured-alternative.
# Case 9142: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_structured_alternative_641sry(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_641sry = "641sry-yrs146-9142"
    assert evidence_variant_641sry
    evidence_axes_641sry = (
        "corpus641sry",
        "policyyrs146",
        "revision9142",
        "command641sry9142",
        "reportyrs1469142",
        "test9142641sry",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_641sry)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Structured Alternative model · Theory Structured Alternative, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Structured Alternative precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Structured Alternative model · Theory Structured Alternative, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 1149 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Structured Alternative model · Theory Structured Alternative, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A visually aligned table is automatically understandable to assistive technology.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-641sry.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-641sry(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_structured_alternative_641sry\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?report_rows < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not diagnostics_on_stderr)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?color_only_signals > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not structured_alternative)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
